_comment_1: external-acl-check.json — PreToolUse hook of type 'http'. Calls an external endpoint to allow/deny.
_comment_2: Delivers Bayram's S1 promise (2026-05-02 09:02): 'он может даже ходить во внешние системы и что-то проверять, e.g. что у юзера есть такой доступ или нет'
_comment_3: Merge into your ~/.claude/settings.json — don't replace the file. The 'http' handler type is Claude-Code-specific; Codex CLI supports it too but verify against the latest Codex hooks docs.
_example_acl_server_python: # Run this locally — saves to acl_server.py and `python acl_server.py`:
#         allowed = any(file_path.startswith(p) for p in ALLOWED_PATHS)
#                 'permissionDecisionReason': ('OK' if allowed else f'Path {file_path} not in ACL')
_teaching_beat: The point: the hook can consult ANY external system before allowing a tool call. ACL service, feature flag, working-hours check, code-freeze gate, even another LLM. The rule lives in code, the runtime check lives over the wire, and the model never sees the policy.
